title: Why I Started MCC Solutions
body:
I work a shift at Lowe's, then I go home and run a licensed financial services business. Most people would probably say that's backwards. I think it's just the only way I knew how to actually start.

### The Plan Nobody Hands You

I graduated with a degree in Accounting and Finance, 3.32 GPA, ready to do the thing everyone tells you to do. Get an entry level job. Build experience. Work your way up. That's still the plan honestly. I'm applying to Junior Accountant and AP Coordinator roles right now, today, as I write this.

But applying and waiting isn't really a full time activity. It's maybe a few hours a week of sending resumes into systems where you're not even sure a person ever sees it. I had the rest of my time, a degree I already paid for, and a bunch of skills nobody was actually using yet.

So in 2026 I started MCC Solutions LLC.

### What It Actually Is

MCC Solutions is a licensed financial services firm. Three things, things I'm already qualified to do right now, not someday. Notary and loan signing work. Bookkeeping. Web design.

I'm a New Jersey Notary Public and an NNA Certified Loan Signing Agent, carrying $100,000 in E&O insurance. QuickBooks ProAdvisor Gold across Level 1, Level 2, and Payroll. Active FINRA SIE and Series 30 licenses, with Series 66 scheduled for May 2026. My EA exam is in July 2026.

None of that needed someone to hire me first. A license is a license whether or not it's attached to a job title.

### Why Not Just Wait For The Real Job

Honest answer. Waiting doesn't build anything. A resume sitting in a tracking system isn't compounding. A business, even a tiny one, is.

Every signing I do is real experience inside an actual real estate closing. Every QuickBooks file I clean up is real bookkeeping work, not a practice exercise. Every license I earn is mine, a job posting can't take it back once I have it. So by the time I do land a W-2 role, I'm not walking in as someone who sat around waiting. I'm walking in as someone who's been doing the work this whole time.

### What This Actually Looks Like

There's no version of building this where it didn't cost something. I work my Lowe's shifts, covering inventory and logistics in the building materials department. Then I do outreach to title companies, follow up on signing platform assignments, and work on client books with whatever's left of the day.

It's not glamorous. Most nights it's a handful of emails sent before bed, not some big breakthrough moment. But this stuff compounds quietly when nobody's watching. The site you're reading this on. The licenses. The insurance. None of it existed eighteen months ago. It exists now because I kept building it during the hours most people spend doing literally anything else after a shift.

### Where This Goes

I don't think MCC Solutions replaces a full time accounting career on its own, at least not yet. The actual plan is both things at once. Keep building the business. Keep going after the W-2 role and the credentials that come with it. Let them feed each other.

A licensed financial services business run by someone actively going for an Enrolled Agent credential and FINRA licensing reads differently than "23 year old's side hustle." That's kind of the point.

I'm writing about this while it's happening, not after the fact, because I think the messy version is more useful to read than the clean one. If you're trying to build something on the side of a job that has nothing to do with where you're actually headed, I see you. It's slower than it looks from the outside. Still worth doing.
